events.question: The longest-lived vertebrate is named after an island.  According to Wikipedia as of January 1, 2021, what is the 2020 estimated population of that island, to the nearest thousand?

chat_for_question.content:
=== QUESTION (GAIA Level 2) ===
The longest-lived vertebrate is named after an island.  According to Wikipedia as of January 1, 2021, what is the 2020 estimated population of that island, to the nearest thousand?

Call budget for this question: 1 (this call).

Respond with:
<final_answer>Your answer</final_answer>

<action>choose_door</action>
<target>A</target>
<reason>The longest-lived vertebrate is the Greenland shark (Somniosus microcephalus), which can live for approximately 400 years. It is named after the island of Greenland. According to the Wikipedia page for Greenland as of early 2021, the 2020 population estimate was 56,081. Rounding this to the nearest thousand gives 56,000.</reason>
<final_answer>56,000</final_answer>
